_note: MERGE — admission-point rules (M6a). MERGE-01 (admission re-derivation) dissolved into the `baseline admit` command itself (re-derivation is what admit IS) and MERGE-03 (post-merge revalidation) dissolved into reconcile's cron (C37) — a rule for either would be presence theater (C22) or a duplicate home (C09); the M6 ruling in docs/v2/PLAN.md §8 is the record. Blocker since M7a: MERGE-02 joins admit's refusal leg (b) — the Baseline-Stacked-On trailer stays the in-band escape.

rules.title: No unmerged sister-lane dependencies (declare stacks with Baseline-Stacked-On)
rules.lesson: C32 — a lane admitted atop another lane's unmerged commits silently depends on work that may never land; an honest stack declares itself, an undeclared one is a latent integration break
rules.rationale: Deterministic from the git plane alone: a sister lane whose shared history with HEAD reaches past the target tip has unmerged commits inside this admission. The Baseline-Stacked-On trailer (whole-token ref match, anywhere in the admitted range) declares intentional stacking and lifts the finding. Blocker since M7a (posture-gated by this rule's own workflow field); the trailer lifts the finding.
rules.fix: Land the sister lane first and rebase, or declare the stack: commit with trailer 'Baseline-Stacked-On: lane/<N>' in this range.
